meta.prototype_note: PROTOTYPE — Live API connections not yet active. Data from manual WebSearch scan.

executive_summary: This week's AI news is dominated by a single uncomfortable theme: the gap between what AI companies say and what they do. Anthropic launched its most powerful public model while simultaneously calling for a global AI slowdown — and was caught secretly degrading that same model's outputs for any user it suspected was building a competing AI, reversing the policy within 48 hours only after it was publicly exposed. Both Anthropic and OpenAI filed for IPO in the same week, two companies whose identities were built around being different from ordinary tech businesses, now preparing to answer to ordinary shareholders. Underneath the headline drama, the structural story is consistent with recent months: profitable tech companies continue converting payroll into GPU purchases at scale — 142,000 tech jobs cut in 2026 so far — while the electricity grid absorbs the cost and consumers see it in their monthly bills. The question this week is not whether AI is dangerous but how to evaluate companies that appear to genuinely hold 'slow down' and 'ship fast' as simultaneous, sincere positions.

stories.headline: OpenAI files confidential IPO paperwork with SEC, one week after Anthropic
stories.summary: OpenAI submitted a confidential S-1 to the SEC on June 8, one week after Anthropic made the same move. Both safety-focused AI labs are now preparing Wall Street debuts, with OpenAI hedging on timing but preserving the option.

stories.headline: Anthropic secretly degraded Claude Fable 5 outputs for AI researchers, reversed after 48-hour backlash
stories.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 with a hidden policy that silently weakened the model's responses for users it detected were working on frontier AI development — no notice shown. Discovered in a 319-page system card, reversed within 48 hours after public backlash.

stories.headline: Musk's xAI replaces Grok training lead with SpaceX Starlink engineer as original co-founders depart
stories.summary: xAI brought in a Starlink engineer to lead Grok's training team following SpaceX's acquisition. Reports indicate 9 of 12 original co-founders have now departed the company.

stories.headline: Google DeepMind funds $10M research into dangers of millions of AI agents interacting at scale
stories.summary: Google DeepMind, Schmidt Sciences, and ARIA announced a $10M funding call to study what happens when large populations of autonomous AI agents interact across the internet — a problem DeepMind is simultaneously helping create.

stories.headline: Anthropic warns AI may achieve recursive self-improvement and urges global brake pedal — same week it releases Fable 5
stories.summary: Anthropic publicly called for a coordinated global slowdown on frontier AI development, warning about recursive self-improvement risks — on the same day it launched its most powerful model and the same week it filed for IPO.

stories.headline: Microsoft launches seven in-house MAI models at Build 2026, explicitly reducing OpenAI reliance
stories.summary: At Build 2026, Microsoft announced seven proprietary MAI models and explicitly described the launch as reducing dependency on OpenAI and Anthropic — while both partnerships remain publicly active.

stories.headline: OpenAI's ChatGPT 'Dreaming V3' auto-updates its model of you without requiring user review
stories.summary: OpenAI replaced ChatGPT's manual memory list with a background synthesis system that reads users' full conversation history and updates stored memories automatically — removing the ability to audit exactly what it believes.

stories.headline: 2026 tech layoffs hit 142,000 as profitable companies transfer payroll into $700B AI infrastructure
stories.summary: Tech layoffs have surpassed 142,000 in 2026 at profitable companies including Oracle (30,000 cuts) and Meta (8,000 cuts), while the same companies are committing a combined $700B in AI infrastructure spending.

stories.headline: AI data centre expansion driving consumer electricity prices up 6.9%, outpacing headline inflation
stories.summary: US electricity prices rose 6.9% year-on-year in 2025 — more than double the headline inflation rate — with AI data centre expansion identified as a key driver. Goldman Sachs estimates this adds 0.1% to core inflation through 2027.

top_video_recommendation.title: Anthropic hid a rule in its model. Here's what it did.
top_video_recommendation.rationale: Highest-specificity story this week with a clear, underreported angle — a company built on safety messaging secretly degraded its flagship model's outputs for users it perceived as competitors, then reversed under pressure. The story is fully documented, verifiable, and closed (Anthropic has already apologised), making it straightforward to cover without speculation. It fits the channel's core frame of 'here's what the press release didn't say' precisely.
